metadata.title: QRNG Art — art from quantum noise
metadata.description: A unique image every day, generated from quantum random numbers. Australian National University, ANU QRNG.
metadata.ogAlt: QRNG Art — art from quantum noise

home.title: Art of the Day
home.paragraph1: This is not a picture drawn by an artist or generated by an AI model. Each day a new image is born solely from quantum randomness — true randomness originating from quantum processes at the Australian National University.
home.paragraph2: The ANU QRNG service generates numbers using photon detection — not a computer algorithm. That means every number is truly unpredictable and unique.
home.paragraph3: Each of the 1000 numbers influences the final image: sky color, number of stars, mountain shape, sun or moon position, clouds. The same set of numbers always produces the same image — reproducible art from chaos.
home.paragraph4Suffix: lets you experiment with your own numbers.

algorithmic.title: Algorithmic Art
algorithmic.subtitle: QRNG values as the engine of generative algorithms
algorithmic.errorRandom: Unable to load QRNG values. Please try again.
algorithmic.errorToday: Unable to load today's art. Please try again.
algorithmic.storyTitle: QRNG story for the selected scene
algorithmic.storyIntro: Each of the 1000 uint16 values from the ANU QRNG batch is read in order through the stream. The palette and sliders do not consume quantum numbers — they only shape the algorithm. Quantum randomness seeds initial angles, positions, per-cell color jitter, and other starting conditions. When an algorithm needs more than 1000 draws, each extra value is produced by XOR-ing a deterministic PRNG output with the original QRNG values cycling from the start — quantum entropy stays present throughout the entire stream, and the same batch always yields the same image.
algorithmic.storyFlowField: Controls: 5 (palette + particles, speed, trail width, trail opacity). From the batch: 336 field angles (24×14 grid) + 4 values per particle for start position, age, and lifespan — total 336 + 4×particle count (e.g. 600 particles ≈ 2736 draws).
algorithmic.storyWave: Controls: 4 (palette + wave count, amplitude, contrast). From the batch: 4 values per wave (frequencies, phase, wave amplitude) + 1 for glow count + 3 per glow — roughly 4×n + 10 to 19 draws, where n is the wave count (4–24).
algorithmic.storyVoronoi: Controls: 3 (palette + cell count, border width). From the batch: 5 values per Voronoi site — RGB jitter and x,y — total 5×n, with n from 20 to 400.
algorithmic.storyLSystem: Controls: 4 (palette + depth, angle, length factor). From the batch: 1 for a slight horizontal offset + 2 per F segment (angle and length jitter) + 1 per + or − symbol. Draw count grows quickly with depth (e.g. depth 5 ≈ 730, depth 7 ≈ 6500).
algorithmic.storyCellular: Controls: 3 (palette + rule, cell size). From the batch: one value per column of the initial row — column count is canvas width divided by cell size (up to about 1200 at the smallest cell size).
algorithmic.storyTruchet: Controls: 3 (palette + tile size, line width). From the batch: one draw per tile for rotation (0° or 90°). If the total tile count is below ~800, an extra draw per tile decides a rare glow overlay.
algorithmic.storyJulia: Controls: 3 (palette + max iterations, zoom). From the batch: exactly the first 4 values — two for the real and imaginary parts of c, two for a small perturbation.
algorithmic.storyNewton: Controls: 4 (palette + polynomial degree, max iterations, zoom). From the batch: exactly 2×degree successive values for initial root positions in the complex plane (degree 3–7 → 6 to 14 draws).
algorithmic.storyApollonian: Apollonian gasket: repeatedly add a circle tangent to three existing ones (Descartes’ theorem and complex-number centers). Starts from the classic −1, 2, 2, 3 curvature quadruple; the first 12 uint16 values gently perturb bends and centers. BFS expands triples until max circle count or minimum radius. Colour uses depth plus a pattern from the batch. Background on the geometry: https://www.malinc.se/math/geometry/apolloniangasketen.php
